steps.action: Code review: search for centralized color definitions
steps.expected: All colors defined as named constants in one location
steps.observed: system/libraries/drawing/palette.rs contains 13 named color constants forming a complete palette: BG_BASE (18,18,26), BG_CONTENT (24,24,36), CHROME_BG (30,30,48,a220), CHROME_BORDER (60,60,80,a200), TEXT_PRIMARY (200,210,230), TEXT_CURSOR (100,180,255), TEXT_SELECTION (50,80,160,a180), CHROME_TITLE (200,200,220), CHROME_SUBTITLE (90,90,110), CHROME_STATUS (130,130,150), CHROME_CLOCK (160,170,190), CHROME_ICON (180,190,220), SHADOW_PEAK (0,0,0,a80), SHADOW_ZERO (0,0,0,a0). Included via include!('palette.rs') in drawing/lib.rs. Design documented as 'dark theme with cool blue-grey tones'.
Boot screenshot shows cohesive dark blue-grey theme. Background near-black with blue cast, translucent chrome panels, light blue-grey text, blue cursor accent. All elements visually harmonious.

evidence.codeReview: palette.rs: 13 named constants covering all UI elements (background, chrome, text, cursor, selection, shadows, status). Centralized in one file.

steps.action: Code review: verify font sizes and line heights
steps.observed: FONT_SIZE=16 for both mono and proportional. Line height computed as size_px + size_px/4 = 16 + 4 = 20 (1.25x font size, satisfies >= 1.2x). Chrome text uses proportional font (Nunito Sans) via prop_cache. Editor text uses monospace (Source Code Pro) via mono_cache.
Screenshot shows proportional 'Document OS' in title bar and 'Editor process active | 0 chars' in status bar. Monospace text in editor. Both legible, consistent sizing within their contexts.

evidence.codeReview: FONT_SIZE=16, line_height=20 (1.25x). prop_cache for chrome, mono_cache for editor. Verified in compositor/main.rs.

steps.observed: TEXT_INSET_X=12px (horizontal text margin). TEXT_INSET_TOP = TITLE_BAR_H(36) + SHADOW_DEPTH(8) + 8 = 52px (text starts well below title bar). TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM = STATUS_BAR_H(28) + SHADOW_DEPTH(8) + 8 = 44px. Chrome text vertically centered within bars. All margins exceed 8px minimum.

steps.expected: Balanced layout, no cramping or wasteful spacing
steps.observed: Screenshot shows well-balanced layout. Title bar has comfortable padding around text and icon. Content area has clean margins from chrome. Status bar text has adequate padding. Overall layout feels intentional and balanced.

evidence.codeReview: TEXT_INSET_X=12, TEXT_INSET_TOP=52, TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM=44. TITLE_BAR_H=36, STATUS_BAR_H=28. All margins >= 8px.

steps.action: Inspect high-quality boot screenshot for boundary artifacts
steps.expected: No gaps, black pixels, or wrong-colored pixels at boundaries
steps.observed: At high resolution, title bar transitions smoothly to content area via drop shadow gradient (8px). Content area transitions smoothly to status bar via upward shadow gradient. No visible gaps, black lines, or color discontinuities at any boundary. Chrome border lines (CHROME_BORDER) provide clean visual separation without artifacts.

screenshots: qemu-screen-boot.png (high quality, inspected boundary regions between title bar, content, and status bar)

summary: All 4 polish assertions pass. Color palette is centralized in palette.rs with 13 named constants forming a cohesive dark blue-grey theme. Typography uses 16px font with 1.25x line height for both mono and proportional. Layout has consistent margins (all >= 8px, text insets 12-52px). No boundary artifacts — drop shadows provide seamless transitions between chrome and content.
